stimulus:
While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes:

  • In 1990, Cree communities in northern Quebec staged protests against the proposed Great Whale hydroelectric project.
  • The project would have flooded traditional hunting and trapping grounds.
  • The project was ultimately shelved, but the Great Whale campaign had a lasting effect.
  • It drew international attention to Indigenous land rights.
  • It contributed to a 2002 agreement formally recognizing Cree authority over local resource management.
stem: The student wants to make and support a generalization about the Great Whale campaign. Which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal?

answerOptions.content: During the Great Whale campaign, Cree communities staged protests to block a hydroelectric project that would have flooded hunting and trapping grounds.
Although the hydroelectric project that the Cree had protested was eventually shelved, the Great Whale campaign had a lasting effect.
The Great Whale campaign had a lasting effect, drawing international attention to Indigenous land rights and contributing to formal recognition of Cree authority over local resources.
Cree authority over local resource management received international attention and legal recognition as a result of the Great Whale campaign.

rationale:
Choice C is the best answer because it makes the generalization (the campaign had a lasting effect) and supports it with two outcomes drawn from the notes.

Choices A and B describe the campaign without making and supporting a generalization. Choice D states an outcome but doesn't frame it as a generalization with support.
